title: Media Tower Cabinet
summary: A tall single-door cabinet with six fixed shelves, built from edge-banded plywood with pocket-hole joinery throughout.
description:
A media tower is really just a very tall, very narrow bookcase with a door on it — six shelves stacked in a 3/4" plywood box, hinged on whichever side makes sense for the room.

The **shelves sit flush with the back panel but set back an inch from the front edge** of the sides — that's not a styling choice, it's what gives the door somewhere to close against without hitting shelf edges. Get that reveal consistent on every shelf and the door closes clean; skip it and you'll be planing shelf edges after the fact.

Edge-band every visible plywood edge before assembly, not after. It's a five-minute job per board on a bench and a miserable one once the cabinet is standing up.

tools.note: Or a track/circular saw for breaking down the sheets first.

materials.name: Primer and paint (or stain and sealer)

cutList.note: Set back 1" from the front edge of the sides, flush with the back — that reveal is what the door closes against.
Can be hinged on either side.

steps.title: Break down the sheets and edge-band
steps.body: Cut the side panels, bottom, back, shelves, top, and door to size from the plywood sheets. Apply edge banding to every edge that will show once assembled, and trim it flush before you move on — this is much easier on flat boards than on a standing cabinet.

steps.title: Attach the bottom
steps.body: Drill pocket holes in the side edges of the bottom panel and attach it to the two sides with glue and pocket screws, set 1-1/2" up from the bottom edge of the sides.

steps.title: Add the bottom supports and back
steps.body: Attach the two bottom support strips underneath the bottom panel, into the sides. Then attach the back panel to the sides and bottom with pocket holes and glue.

steps.title: Install the five shelves
steps.body:
Attach each shelf flush with the back panel — which automatically sets it back 1" from the front edge of the sides — gluing and pocket-screwing through the sides and back at each one.

Keep that 1" reveal identical on all five shelves. An inconsistent reveal is what makes a door hang crooked later.

steps.title: Attach the top
steps.body: Screw and glue the top panel onto the assembled carcass.

steps.title: Hang the door and finish
steps.body: Attach the two hinges to the door — either side works — and hang it on the cabinet. Add the cabinet pull, then prime and paint, or stain and seal.
materials: Primer and paint (or stain and sealer)
